meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 2)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Practice recognizing proper nouns including days, months, and holidays.

questions.question: Which of these is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Monday" is a proper noun — days of the week are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: "Monkey" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "monkey" refers to any monkey — that's a common noun.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I live in new taipei city.
I live in New Taipei City.
I live in new Taipei City.
i live in New Taipei City.
questions.explanation: "New Taipei City" is a proper noun — all words in a place name must be capitalized.

questions.question: "January" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! January is a month name — months are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "teacher" is a common noun — it refers to any teacher, not a specific person.

questions.question: What type of noun is "March"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: March is a proper noun — months are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: city/park/teacher = general words (common). Sharon/Christmas = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "I live in New Taipei City," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! New Taipei City is a proper noun — all words in this place name are correctly capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
questions.explanation: "March" is a month name (proper noun) and must be capitalized. "birthday" is a common noun.

questions.question: We sing songs on ___ (the holiday, write correctly). Clue: The word should be 'Christmas'.
questions.hint: This holiday name must start with a capital C
questions.explanation: Christmas is a proper noun (holiday name) — it must be capitalized: Christmas.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "It is very hot in July." July is a proper noun (month).

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: girl/hospital = general words (common). Monday/Taipei/January = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "The library is very quiet," the word "library" should be capitalized.
questions.explanation: "library" is a common noun — it stays lowercase (unless it's a specific library name like 'Taipei City Library').

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: We go to the park on saturday.
We go to the park on Saturday.
We Go To The Park On Saturday.
We go to the Park on Saturday.
questions.explanation: Only "Saturday" needs a capital — it's a proper noun. "park" is a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Friday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: Days of the week are always capitalized
questions.explanation: Friday is a proper noun — days of the week are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "This summer I will take a trip to America." America is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which word in this list IS a common noun?
questions.explanation: "newspaper" is a common noun — it refers to any newspaper. The others are proper nouns.

questions.question: In the sentence "We love going to jiufen village," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: "Jiufen Village" is a proper noun (a specific place name) — both words should be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: song/corn/dog = general words (common). Taiwan/Dragon Boat Festival = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix this proper noun: 'daan park'. Write it correctly.
questions.hint: Both words in a place name need capital letters
questions.explanation: "Daan Park" is a proper noun — both words start with capital letters.
